overrides.reason: Codex uses a TOML MCP configuration example; canonical MCP metadata remains in .agents/mcp/servers.json.
Codex permission prompts are represented as rule prefixes generated from .agents/permissions.json.
Claude Code discovers project instructions through CLAUDE.md, which delegates back to AGENTS.md.
Claude Code stores project settings in .claude/settings.json; generated settings expose permission intent without duplicating repository guidance.
Claude Code reads project MCP servers from .mcp.json; generated content is derived from .agents/mcp/servers.json.
Claude skills are exposed through a generated symlink to .agents/skills to avoid duplicate canonical skill content.

opencode reads AGENTS.md for rules and discovers skills natively from .agents/skills, so opencode.json only carries generated MCP servers and a permission.bash mapping derived from .agents/permissions.json.
Kiro consumes steering files; generated steering explicitly delegates to AGENTS.md and .agents.
Kiro MCP settings are generated from canonical MCP metadata.
Kiro custom agent (JSON) generated from project.json + permissions.json. Its filename uses the project name; it maps git permissions to toolsSettings.shell. The Kiro default agent is unaffected.
Kiro discovers skills under .kiro/skills via a generated symlink to .agents/skills so skill bodies load on demand instead of duplicating guidance in AGENTS.md.
Antigravity support is conservative while its project settings surface changes; generated files point back to AGENTS.md and require local validation.
